titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale University Buildings and Grounds Photographs RU.703
Yale University Buildings and Grounds Photographs
titlestmt.author: compiled by Manuscripts and Archives Staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday July 12, 2019 at 07:54
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: Yale University buildings and grounds photographs
did.abstract: The collection contains pictorial materials depicting various Yale buildings and grounds. Included are maps, aerial views of Yale and New Haven, and photographic prints of architectural plans, drawings and proposals. Materials for many buildings are classified in individual record units and are not described here.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 46-A (YRG 46-A), Records and materials broadly documenting Yale buildings, facilities, and grounds.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale University Buildings and Grounds Photographs (RU 703). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains pictorial materials depicting various Yale buildings and grounds, and is arranged according to a region and chronology identification system. Included are maps, aerial views of Yale and New Haven, and photographic prints of architectural plans, drawings and proposals. Materials for many buildings are classified in individual record units and are not described here.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in five series and subsequent accessions. I. Photographs and drawings. II. Oversize. III. Slides. IV. Transparencies. V. Folio.

odd.p: Includes two slides of Connecticut Hall.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seven mounted prints of exterior and interior.

Photographs of Yale buildings and grounds
Photographs of Yale buildings and grounds
Hand colored drawing of York Hall
Hand colored drawing of York Hall, now Stoeckel Hall, 96 Wall Street, designed by Grosvenor Atterbury. Original uncolored drawing was published in "American Architect and Building News," No. 1120
Preliminary presentation drawings for accepted design for Yale Library by C.C. Haight
Photogravure of Yale University by Richard Rummell
Photogravure of bird's-eye view of Yale University, drawn and signed by Richard Rummell (1848-1924) and published by F. D. Nichols, Boston
